texto: What is literature, and why do I try to write about it? I don’t know. Likewise, I don’t know why I go on living, most of the time. But this not knowing is precisely what I want to preserve. As readers, the closest way we can engage with a literary work is to protect its indeterminacy; to return ourselves and it to a place that precludes complete recognition. Really, when I’m reading, all I want is to stand amazed in front of an unknown object at odds with the world.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de M. John Harrison que provoca passagem do tempo e prioridades. Ao questionar “What is literature, and why do I try to write about it? I don’t know. Likewise, I don’t know why …”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre vida, interview, light, com foco em trabalho e light.

But this not knowing is precisely what I want to preserve. As readers, the closest way we can engage with a literary work is to protect its indeterminacy; to return ourselves and it to a place that precludes complete recognition. Really, when I’m reading, all I want is to stand amazed in front of an unknown object at odds with the world.

texto: Egnaro is a secret known to everyone but yourself.It is a country or a city to which you have never been; it is an unknown language. At the same time it is like being cuckolded, or plotted against. It is part of the universe of events which will never wholly reveal itself to you: a conspiracy the barest outline of which, once visible, will gall you forever.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a M. John Harrison: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “Egnaro is a secret known to everyone but yourself.It is a country or a city to which you have nev…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que autoestima, fantasy, paranoia pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de paranoia.

texto: Happiness and beauty are the worst things you can have in a life, because you never forget them. They go on and on ambushing you, presumably until you die.
explicacao: Citação de M. John Harrison sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. “Happiness and beauty are the worst things you can have in a life, because you never forget them. …” articula felicidade, loss, memory em registro de felicidade, com relevância para contextos como luto e memory.

texto: Dreamworlds can maintain themselves only as glimpses. Once the writer transports the reader across the threshold, nothing that was promised can be delivered. What was ominous becomes ordinary; what was bizarre, quotidian. Unless you simply keep upping the ante, piling on the bullshit, the only way to revive things is to switch perspectives as quickly as you can.
explicacao: Citação de M. John Harrison sobre sentido da existência e experiência vivida. “Dreamworlds can maintain themselves only as glimpses. Once the writer transports the reader acros…” articula motivacao, fantasy, writing em registro de motivacao, com relevância para contextos como writing.
